title: Before implementing, briefly analyze: which files need changes, potential breaking changes, edge cases. Then implement the task. Task: Review failed: review score 4/10 (min 7): If score < 7, list specific issues to fix (max 5 items).
summary_text: Files that needed changes were the verifier itself and a small focused test harness: [assets/scripts/canon-v3-verifier.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/scripts/canon-v3-verifier.sh#L29) and [assets/scripts/test-canon-v3-verifier.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/scripts/test-canon-v3-verifier.sh#L1). The main risk was changing the verifier output contract too much and breaking the pending-fix consumer, so I kept the queue format unchanged and only made the review prompt and issue extraction stricter. The main edge cases were prompt-echo text being misread as findings, prose-only low-score reviews, and accidental issue capture on high-score reviews. Implemented: - [assets/scripts/canon-v3-verifier.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/scripts/canon-v3-verifier.sh#L29) now prefers an explicit `ISSUES TO FIX:` section, normalizes items to bullets, and caps them at 5. - [assets/scripts/canon-v3-verifier.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/scripts/canon-v3-verifier.sh#L191) now instructs the reviewer to emit that section only when score `< 7`. - [assets/scripts/canon-v3-verifier.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/scripts/canon-v3-verifier.sh#L220) now includes an `issues to fix:` heading in the queued failure output. - [assets/scripts/canon-v3-verifier.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/scripts/canon-v3-verifier.sh#L304) is wrapped in `main()` so the parser can be sourced safely for tests. - [assets/scripts/test-canon-v3-verifier.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/scripts/test-canon-v3-verifier.sh#L34) covers explicit-section parsing, bullet fallback cleanup, high-score empty output, and 5-item capping. Verified with `bash -n assets/scripts/canon-v3-verifier.sh` and `bash assets/scripts/test-canon-v3-verifier.sh`.
